testcases.input: nums = [0,1,2] Output: 0 Explanation: i=0: 0 mod 10 = 0 == nums[0]. i=1: 1 mod 10 = 1 == nums[1]. i=2: 2 mod 10 = 2 == nums[2]. All indices have i mod 10 == nums[i], so we return the smallest index 0.
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: i=0: 0 mod 10 = 0 == nums[0]. i=1: 1 mod 10 = 1 == nums[1]. i=2: 2 mod 10 = 2 == nums[2]. All indices have i mod 10 == nums[i], so we return the smallest index 0.

testcases.input: nums = [4,3,2,1] Output: 2 Explanation: i=0: 0 mod 10 = 0 != nums[0]. i=1: 1 mod 10 = 1 != nums[1]. i=2: 2 mod 10 = 2 == nums[2]. i=3: 3 mod 10 = 3 != nums[3]. 2 is the only index which has i mod 10 == nums[i].
testcases.expectedOutput: 2 Explanation: i=0: 0 mod 10 = 0 != nums[0]. i=1: 1 mod 10 = 1 != nums[1]. i=2: 2 mod 10 = 2 == nums[2]. i=3: 3 mod 10 = 3 != nums[3]. 2 is the only index which has i mod 10 == nums[i].

testcases.input: nums = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9,0] Output: -1 Explanation: No index satisfies i mod 10 == nums[i].
testcases.expectedOutput: -1 Explanation: No index satisfies i mod 10 == nums[i].
